title: Prenatal screening: what parents should know
excerpt: Purpose of screening tests, common options, questions to ask, and limits of results — not a substitute for genetic counseling.
content:
## Purpose

Screening estimates risk for certain chromosomal and structural conditions; it is not diagnostic. Positive screens lead to further testing discussions.

## Commonly offered (varies by country)

- First-trimester combined screening, NIPT cell-free DNA, anatomy ultrasound, glucose screening.

## What you can do

- Ask what each test detects, false-positive rates, timing, and costs.
- Bring partner or support person to counseling visits.

## Notes

Results can cause anxiety — genetic counselors help interpret choices. Declining tests is a valid informed choice in many systems.

metaDescription: What prenatal screening is for, common tests, and how to prepare for appointments.
